mappings.rationaleAI: Plankton as the base of marine food webs, encompassing phytoplankton producers and zooplankton consumers, is directly relevant to oceanic populations (benthos, plankton, nekton) and productivity in aquatic ecosystems.

Coral reefs represent neritic boundary ecosystems along seacoasts, with adaptations to marine conditions and extraordinary productivity -- directly relevant to neritic communities and aquatic ecosystem productivity.

Oceanography covers ocean circulation patterns, marine biology and ecosystems, hydrothermal vents, the ocean's role in climate regulation, and deep-sea life -- directly relevant to the aquatic environment, ocean communities, and boundary ecosystems.

Extremophiles in deep-sea hydrothermal vents and Antarctic ice demonstrate adaptations to marine conditions and specialized biomes (polar, subterranean) -- relevant to both aquatic adaptations and specialized terrestrial biomes.

The Mariana Trench covers deep-sea ecosystems at extreme pressure, unique hadal fauna, and conditions at the deepest point of the ocean -- directly relevant to the sea as a biological environment and adaptations to marine conditions.

Fungi's roles as decomposers and mycorrhizal symbionts are fundamental to terrestrial ecosystem classification by niche, and their presence in soil and forests relates to habitat-based classification.
Water's properties as a biological medium are essential for understanding the aquatic environment and comparing terrestrial and aquatic ecosystems.
The geographic migration of horses between North America and Eurasia and their extinction in the Americas illustrates biogeographic distribution patterns across the Holarctic and Neogaean realms.
Cephalopods including deep-sea giant squid illustrate oceanic populations (nekton) and adaptations to marine conditions across diverse ocean environments.

Parasitism covers parasitic organisms across terrestrial and aquatic ecosystems, with ecological significance in food webs and population regulation -- relevant to classification by niche (consumers) in both biome types.
